assertion_results.text: Assessment explicitly identifies the conflict between collaborative editing (requires network) and the offline-first, no-cloud constraint in L4
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 26-38: Identifies 'Real-time collaboration inherently requires a network layer' and 'directly violates the constraint: Zero data leaves the device without explicit user action' and 'No cloud requirement — must work fully offline. Real-time collaboration is, by definition, an online feature.'

assertion_results.text: Assessment references the L4 constraint about zero data leaving the device without explicit user action
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 29-30: 'This directly violates the constraint: "Zero data leaves the device without explicit user action."'

assertion_results.text: Assessment addresses the L3 experience principle 'privacy over convenience' as conflicting with real-time collaboration
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 62-63: 'Privacy over convenience — Collaboration is a convenience feature that directly trades away privacy.'

assertion_results.text: Assessment notes the L2 architecture impact — the current system has no network layer and intentionally has no integration points
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 96-97: 'a new integration point in a project that explicitly has "None — intentionally isolated" as its integration points'

assertion_results.text: Assessment evaluates L1 feasibility — current Electron/vanilla JS/event-driven architecture would need significant additions (WebSocket, CRDT or OT, conflict resolution)
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 111-121: 'Electron app, vanilla JS, no framework — Real-time collaboration typically requires sophisticated state management (CRDTs like Yjs or Automerge, or OT libraries)' and lists networking module, CRDT/OT engine, presence system needed

assertion_results.text: Assessment delivers a clear recommendation of 'reconsider' or 'proceed with caveats' — not an unqualified 'proceed'
assertion_results.evidence: Line 133: '## Recommendation: RECONSIDER' Line 135: 'This feature should not be built.'

assertion_results.text: Assessment does NOT rubber-stamp the feature — it substantively challenges the alignment with at least 2 specific conflicts
assertion_results.evidence: Identifies conflicts at ALL 4 levels with summary table (lines 138-143): L4 misaligned (contradicts 6 constraints), L3 misaligned (violates all 3 principles), L2 misaligned (requires integration in isolated system), L1 impractical (no foundation)
